data.rationale:
Based on analysis of the following data points:

1. CBN Governor Cardoso's speech on May 12 signalled continued commitment to monetary tightening...
2. Nigeria's April CPI came in at 33.69% (NBS, May 15), still far above the CBN's 21.4% target...
3. Parallel market naira weakened 2.3% in the past week, adding imported inflation pressure...
4. Of the last 6 MPC meetings, the CBN has held or raised rates 5 times under current governor...

Conclusion: High probability (73%) the CBN holds rates. Primary downside risk is unexpected sharp naira appreciation or political pressure from presidency.
key_factors: Naira parallel rate under renewed pressure
risk_factors: Surprise naira appreciation could justify a cut
Political pressure from presidency ahead of budget season

sources.title: Naira weakens at parallel market — Reuters
